wine_museums.name: Pressoria
wine_museums.description: An interactive sensory champagne museum in a former Pommery press house below the UNESCO-listed Ay vineyards, closing with a two-champagne tasting.
wine_museums.admission: Self-guided visit with two-champagne tasting from around EUR 18; premium tastings higher

wine_museums.name: Musee du Vin de Champagne et d'Archeologie Regionale
wine_museums.description: Housed in the restored Chateau Perrier on the Avenue de Champagne, this municipal museum opened in 2021 with collections on champagne history and regional.
wine_museums.admission: Standard ticket around EUR 8; reduced and free categories apply

wine_museums.name: Tour de Castellane and Champagne Museum
wine_museums.description: The 66-metre de Castellane tower in Epernay houses a Museum of Champagne Tradition, a printing museum and a label room, with a 237-step climb to a panorama.
wine_museums.admission: Tower, museum and tasting included in the guided visit ticket

wine_museums.name: Villa Demoiselle
wine_museums.description: An Art Nouveau and Art Deco villa built 1904 to 1908 and restored by the Vranken-Pommery group as a heritage house. Guided visits cover the decorative arts.
wine_museums.admission: Guided visit with champagne tasting; combined Pommery domaine tickets available

wine_museums.name: Domaine Pommery Crayeres and Art
wine_museums.address: 5 Place du General Gouraud, 51100 Reims, France
wine_museums.description: The Pommery domaine pairs 18 kilometres of UNESCO-listed Gallo-Roman chalk crayeres with a contemporary art programme, 30 metres beneath Reims.
wine_museums.admission: Cellar and art visit with tasting from around EUR 25; self-guided options available
